<!-- Shared include rendered on BOTH the Windows and Mac setup pages.
     Platform-neutral only: what a package is, install vs. load, and the
     GitHub-phase framing. Edit once here; both pages update. Anything
     platform-specific (Rtools, XQuartz, build tools) belongs on its own page,
     not in this include. -->

Using any R package is really two steps that are easy to blur together.
**Installing** downloads the package onto your computer — you do this once.
**Loading** switches it on for your current session — you do this each time you
start R, with `library()`. (Think of installing as buying a book for your shelf,
and loading as taking it down to read.)

jstats is currently distributed through **GitHub** — a website where software is
shared and developed — rather than from **CRAN**, the central archive you
downloaded R from. That difference is the only reason your computer needs a little
one-time preparation now, and why the install command on the next page looks
slightly different from an ordinary package install. Once jstats reaches CRAN,
that preparation falls away and installing becomes a single ready-made download.
Until then, this page covers the small bit your computer needs.
